title: Resource Downloads & Digital Credential
objectives: Download the U.S. Market Entry Checklist, Florida Setup Roadmap, Central Florida Resource Guide, and Expansion Decision Worksheet
Understand the POK Tech digital credential and how to display it on LinkedIn

contentBlocks.title: Your Takeaway Resources
contentBlocks.content:
Now that you have completed your readiness self-assessment, make sure you leave with the right tools. These four downloadable resources turn what you learned into step-by-step action:

  1. U.S. Market Entry Checklist . Module-by-module action list from legal setup to ecosystem resources.
  2. Florida Setup Roadmap . Step-by-step sequence from entity formation to fully operational.
  3. Central Florida Resource Guide . Directory of organizations, contacts, and programs for international founders.
  4. Expansion Decision Worksheet . Structured go/no-go framework to consider whether now is the right time.

Here is the practical takeaway. Download all four now. They pair directly with your 1:1 meeting with UCF BIP.

contentBlocks.title: Resource 1: U.S. Market Entry Checklist
contentBlocks.content:
The U.S. Market Entry Checklist converts every major topic from this program into action items organized by module: legal setup, finances, IP protection, team building, market entry, culture, and ecosystem resources. Each item includes a priority level and suggested timeframe.

What does that mean for you? Tip: Start with the ecosystem section — it connects you with free resources before you start paying for services.

contentBlocks.title: Resource 2: Florida Setup Roadmap
contentBlocks.content:
The Florida Setup Roadmap organizes by sequence instead of topic : answering "what do I do first, second, third?" It walks through four phases: (1) pre-arrival preparation (remote), (2) entity formation and registration, (3) financial infrastructure, and (4) operational readiness. Each phase includes estimated timeframes and flags which steps require physical presence versus remote completion.

The bottom line? Many founders complete phases 1 and 2 remotely before arriving, so they can open a bank account in one visit with all documents ready.

contentBlocks.title: Resource 3: Central Florida Resource Guide
contentBlocks.content:
The Central Florida Resource Guide puts all the organizations from Module 7 into a single, searchable reference. It covers business advising (SBDC, SCORE, UCF BIP), incubators and accelerators, government programs (SBA, Enterprise Florida), professional services (attorneys, CPAs, banking contacts), networking and community organizations, and UCF BIP phases 2 and 3 details.

Let's put this in perspective. Each entry includes the organization name, what they offer, and their website. Bookmark it and update it as you discover new resources through networking.

contentBlocks.title: Resource 4: Expansion Decision Worksheet
contentBlocks.content:
The Expansion Decision Worksheet helps you decide whether to enter the U.S. market now. It scores you across five dimensions: market fit, financial readiness, legal/regulatory readiness, operational capacity, and timing/opportunity cost.

Here is the thing. This is not pass/fail. A founder from Colombia scored 2 out of 5 on financial readiness, spent four months strengthening home-market revenue, re-scored at 4, and entered the U.S. with a much stronger foundation. The worksheet tells you what gap to close first.

Complete this before your 1:1 meeting with UCF BIP — it helps the team give you targeted advice based on your specific readiness profile.

contentBlocks.title: Your Digital Credential
contentBlocks.content:
When you complete this program, you earn a digital credential : a verified badge for your LinkedIn profile, website, or email signature. It validates completion of the UCF BIP U.S. Market Readiness Program.

Now, here is where it gets tricky. The credential is issued through POK Tech, the same provider used by Arizona State University and other major universities. It is a program completion credential, not a professional certification — but it signals you took a structured, university-backed program seriously.

How to Claim Your Credential

After completing the program, you receive an email from POK Tech. The process takes five minutes:

  1. Open the email and select the claim link
  2. Create your POK Tech account (or log in)
  3. Accept the credential
  4. Share to LinkedIn using the built-in sharing tool

The credential does not expire.

questions.stem: A founder has validated her market opportunity and wants to know the correct ORDER of steps to become operational in Florida — from entity formation through financial setup to hiring. Which downloadable resource is specifically designed for this purpose?
options: U.S. Market Entry Checklist — it covers every topic from the program in checklist format
Florida Setup Roadmap — it organizes steps by sequence (what to do first, second, third) rather than by topic
Expansion Decision Worksheet — it helps assess whether to enter the U.S. market at all
Central Florida Resource Guide — it lists organizations and contacts available to founders
questions.explanation: The Florida Setup Roadmap is organized by sequence rather than by topic. It walks founders through four phases : pre-arrival preparation, entity formation, financial infrastructure, and operational readiness — in the correct order. The Checklist organizes by module topic, while the Roadmap answers 'what do I do first, second, third?'

questions.stem: A founder completed the Expansion Decision Worksheet and scored high on market fit but low on financial readiness. Based on the lesson guidance, what is the most strategic response?
options: Enter the U.S. market immediately since market fit is the most important factor
Abandon U.S. expansion plans entirely since financial readiness is insufficient
Strengthen home-market revenue first to improve financial readiness, then revisit the worksheet before entering the U.S.
Apply for an SBA loan to cover the financial gap before entering the market
questions.explanation: The Expansion Decision Worksheet is not a pass/fail test. A founder from Colombia used it, found her financial score was 2 out of 5, spent four months strengthening home-market revenue, and re-scored at 4 before entering the U.S. with a stronger foundation. The worksheet tells you what gap to close first, not whether to give up.

contentBlocks.title: Planning Your Resource Use
contentBlocks.prompt: Look at the four downloadable resources: the Market Entry Checklist, the Florida Setup Roadmap, the Central Florida Resource Guide, and the Expansion Decision Worksheet. Which one do you need to use first given where you are in your expansion process? Write down the specific resource, the date you plan to work through it, and who (if anyone) you want to review it with : a co-founder, an advisor, or the UCF BIP team during your 1:1.

contentBlocks.title: Key Takeaway
contentBlocks.content: These four resources are yours to keep. They extend the value of this program beyond the screen and into your actual business planning. The checklist keeps you organized. The roadmap keeps you in sequence. The resource guide connects you to help. The decision worksheet keeps you honest about readiness. Download all four now, and bring them to your 1:1 meeting with the UCF BIP team. And claim your digital credential on LinkedIn. It is a small step, but it signals to everyone in your network that you are serious about doing this the right way.
